stories.name: nostr-identity
stories.title: Nostr Identity: Tier 1 Auto-Identity + KeyPackage Publication
stories.description: Add dependencies (NDK, nostr-tools, idb-keyval, marmot-ts@0.4.0). On first app load, auto-generate a Nostr keypair and store it encrypted in localStorage (lp_nostrIdentity_v1). Publish kind 0 metadata (nickname from profile) to default relays. Publish 5 initial kind 443 KeyPackages. Expose identity via NostrIdentityContext (pubkey, npub, signer). Add Tier 1 identity chip to the settings page showing npub (truncated) with a 'Copy npub' button.
acceptance_criteria: On first launch, a Nostr keypair is generated and persisted in localStorage under lp_nostrIdentity_v1
Keypair is consistent across page reloads (same pubkey shown in settings)
5 kind 443 KeyPackages are published to default relays on first launch
Settings page shows identity chip with truncated npub and 'Copy npub' button
NostrIdentityContext is available app-wide and provides pubkey, npub, and signer
make build succeeds
make test-unit passes

includes: app/src/lib/nostrKeys.ts: keypair generation, localStorage persistence (lp_nostrIdentity_v1), npub encoding
app/src/lib/ndkClient.ts: singleton NDK instance, default relay list (relay.damus.io, nos.lol, relay.nostr.band)
app/src/lib/keyPackages.ts: publish 5 kind 443 KeyPackages via NDK, replenish logic
app/pages/settings.tsx: add identity section with npub chip and copy button
app/src/types/index.ts: add NostrIdentity type, default relay constants
app/tests/unit/nostrKeys.test.ts: keypair generation, persistence, npub encoding

stories.notes: Pin marmot-ts to 0.4.0 exactly (no ^). NDK singleton initialized lazily (not on SSR). KeyPackage publishing happens client-side only. Default relays: wss://relay.damus.io, wss://nos.lol, wss://relay.nostr.band. Store private key as hex in localStorage for simplicity (Tier 1 accepts localStorage loss risk). Use nostr-tools nip19.npubEncode for npub encoding.

stories.name: marmot-adapter
stories.title: Marmot Adapter Layer: MarmotClient Context + Group State Storage
stories.description: Create the adapter layer wrapping marmot-ts behind a stable interface. Implement MarmotContext providing createGroup, inviteByNpub, leaveGroup, and sendScoreUpdate operations. Use idb-keyval for IndexedDB group state storage (group list, member scores). Groups are persisted across reloads. Implement the NdkNetworkAdapter implementing NostrNetworkInterface for marmot-ts.
acceptance_criteria: MarmotContext is available app-wide and provides group CRUD operations
NdkNetworkAdapter correctly wraps NDK for marmot-ts relay I/O
Group state persists to IndexedDB (survives page reload)
MarmotProvider initializes without throwing when offline
TypeScript types for Group, GroupMember, ScoreUpdate, MemberScore are defined in src/types/index.ts
make build succeeds
make test-unit passes (adapter unit tests with mocked NDK)

includes: app/src/lib/marmot/groupStorage.ts: idb-keyval wrappers for group list and member scores
app/src/types/index.ts: Group, GroupMember, ScoreUpdate, MemberScore types, STORAGE_KEYS additions
app/tests/unit/marmotAdapter.test.ts: unit tests for groupStorage, NdkNetworkAdapter

stories.notes: NdkNetworkAdapter: implement publish(event), subscribe(filter, onEvent), unsubscribe(subId). groupStorage keys: 'quizzl_groups_v1' (list of Group), 'quizzl_member_scores_v1' (map of groupId -> MemberScore[]). MarmotClient is constructed once in MarmotProvider using the NdkNetworkAdapter and an IndexedDB storage backend. Wrap all marmot-ts calls in try/catch — it's alpha. If marmot-ts does not export a usable IndexedDB storage backend, implement a minimal one using idb-keyval.

stories.name: groups-ui
stories.title: Groups UI: /groups Page + Create Group + Group Detail
stories.description: Add a /groups page to the app. Show list of joined groups (empty state for new users). 'Create Group' button opens a modal for group name input — calls MarmotContext.createGroup(). Each group in the list links to a /groups/[id] detail page showing the group name, member list (npub truncated), and placeholder for member scores. Add 'Groups' to the navigation. Soft-limit: show warning if member count approaches 50.
acceptance_criteria: /groups page is accessible from navigation and shows group list
'Create Group' flow: modal, name input, submit calls createGroup(), new group appears in list
/groups/[id] page shows group name and member list
Empty state shown when user has no groups
Groups persist after page reload (IndexedDB via MarmotContext)
Navigation includes 'Groups' link
make build succeeds
make test-e2e passes for new story-09-groups-ui.spec.ts

includes: app/src/components/groups/MemberList.tsx: member list in detail page

stories.notes: For E2E tests, mock the MarmotContext at the page level using a localStorage-backed stub so tests don't need a live relay. Group detail page shows members as npub chips. Navigation: add 'Groups' between 'Leaderboard' and 'Study Times' in Layout.tsx.

stories.name: invite-and-join
stories.title: Invite by Npub + Auto-Join on Welcome Receipt
stories.description: Implement the full invite/join lifecycle. On the group detail page, add 'Invite Member' button. User pastes an npub. App fetches the invitee's kind 443 KeyPackage from relays. If found, calls group.inviteByKeyPackageEvent() which publishes kind 445 Commit + kind 444 Welcome. On the invitee's side, app subscribes to kind 444 Welcomes on startup and auto-joins groups. Add 'Leave Group' functionality. Show clear error messages for missing KeyPackages.
acceptance_criteria: 'Invite Member' input accepts npub and shows error for invalid format
If invitee has no KeyPackage on relays, show 'User has not set up their Quizzl identity yet' error
If KeyPackage found, invite completes and invitee appears in member list
On receiving a kind 444 Welcome, the group auto-appears in the invitee's groups list after reload
'Leave Group' button removes the group from the local groups list
All operations show loading states and handle errors gracefully
make build succeeds
make test-e2e passes for new story-10-invite-join.spec.ts (using local relay stub)

includes: app/src/components/groups/InviteMemberModal.tsx: npub input, KeyPackage fetch, invite flow
app/src/lib/marmot/welcomeSubscription.ts: subscribe to kind 444 Welcomes, process and auto-join
excludes: Multi-device sync (out of scope per spec)

stories.notes: WelcomeSubscription: start on app load in NostrIdentityContext. On receiving kind 444 addressed to our pubkey: attempt MarmotClient.processWelcome(), if successful add group to groupStorage and trigger UI refresh. Self-update (leaf key rotation) should be called after joining. For E2E, use a local strfry relay via docker-compose.e2e.yml. Invite error messages should be specific: 'Invalid npub format', 'User not found on relays', 'User has not published a KeyPackage'.

stories.name: score-sync
stories.title: Score Sync: Publish ScoreUpdate on Quiz Completion + Receive Member Scores
stories.description: After a user completes a quiz (reaches the completion screen), publish a ScoreUpdate MLS application message to each group the user belongs to. The ScoreUpdate contains topicSlug, quizPoints, maxPoints, completedTasks, totalTasks, lastStudiedAt. Other members subscribe to kind 445 group messages and update their local member score cache (IndexedDB). The group detail page and leaderboard page show group members' scores.
acceptance_criteria: On quiz completion, a ScoreUpdate is sent to all groups the user belongs to
ScoreUpdate includes: topicSlug, quizPoints, maxPoints, completedTasks, totalTasks, lastStudiedAt, sequenceNumber
Members receiving a ScoreUpdate update their local cache (latest by sequence wins)
Group detail page shows each member's total quizPoints across all topics
Leaderboard page: when user is in groups, shows group members alongside the user's own score
Score sync is non-blocking (failures are silently retried, never break quiz flow)
make build succeeds
make test-unit passes (score sync logic)
make test-e2e passes for new story-11-score-sync.spec.ts

includes: app/pages/topic/[slug].tsx: trigger publishScoreUpdate on quiz completion (QuizTab)
app/pages/leaderboard.tsx: add group members section when user is in groups
app/tests/unit/scoreSync.test.ts: buildScoreUpdate, sequence number logic, score merging
excludes: Periodic heartbeat syncing (spec says quiz completion only for this iteration)

stories.notes: ScoreUpdate includes a sequenceNumber (monotonically increasing, stored in localStorage per group). On receive: merge with existing cache using last-writer-wins by sequenceNumber. publishScoreToGroups is fire-and-forget (no await in quiz flow). Queue updates if group is mid-epoch-transition (catch MLS errors and retry on next quiz completion). The leaderboard shows group members as additional entries below the user's own entry with a 'From group: {groupName}' label.

stories.name: seed-phrase-backup
stories.title: Tier 2: Seed Phrase Backup + Identity Recovery
stories.description: Add seed phrase (BIP-39 mnemonic) backup flow to the settings page. User can generate a mnemonic from their existing private key. Show it once with 'Write this down' prompt and a confirmation checkbox. On a fresh install, user can restore identity by entering a mnemonic. Store a flag (lp_nostrIdentityBackedUp_v1) when backup is confirmed. Show a non-intrusive reminder banner when user joins their first group without having backed up.
acceptance_criteria: 'Back up your identity' section in Settings shows 'Generate backup phrase' button
Clicking generates a BIP-39 mnemonic (12 words) derived from the existing private key
Mnemonic is shown exactly once with a 'I have written this down' checkbox that must be checked to proceed
After confirmation, lp_nostrIdentityBackedUp_v1 is set to true
'Restore from backup' flow: enter mnemonic, restores the keypair, re-publishes kind 0 and KeyPackages
When user is in a group but has not backed up, a dismissible reminder banner appears on the groups page
make build succeeds
make test-unit passes (mnemonic generation + restore)
make test-e2e passes for new story-12-seed-phrase.spec.ts

includes: app/src/lib/bip39.ts: mnemonicFromHex, hexFromMnemonic (use nostr-tools or implement BIP39 directly)
app/pages/groups.tsx: show BackupReminderBanner if in groups + not backed up
excludes: Multi-device restoration of MLS group state

stories.notes: For BIP-39: use the @scure/bip39 library (or implement a minimal wordlist). Check if nostr-tools exports BIP39 helpers before adding a new dep. The mnemonic is derived from the 32-byte private key using entropy-to-mnemonic. Restoration: decode mnemonic back to hex private key, replace lp_nostrIdentity_v1, re-run initial setup (kind 0 + KeyPackages). Show mnemonic in a masked/blurred state that requires hover/click to reveal (privacy). STORAGE_KEYS: add nostrIdentity: 'lp_nostrIdentity_v1', nostrBackedUp: 'lp_nostrIdentityBackedUp_v1'.

stories.name: offline-resilience-polish
stories.title: Offline Resilience + Groups Polish: Offline Indicators, Error States, UI Polish
stories.description: Add online/offline detection for group operations. When offline, group features show a clear 'offline' indicator with a 'last synced' timestamp. Score sync failures are queued and retried when connectivity returns. Ensure all solo learning features (quiz, notes, study plan) work fully offline. Polish the groups UI: member count badge on group cards, soft-limit warning at 45+ members, accessible group list with keyboard navigation, responsive layout on mobile. Extend resetAllData to clear IndexedDB group state.
acceptance_criteria: When offline, group pages show an 'Offline - groups unavailable' banner with last-sync timestamp
Solo learning (quiz, notes, study plan) works fully offline without any group-related errors
Score sync failures are queued in localStorage (lp_scoreSyncQueue_v1) and retried on reconnect
resetAllData() clears lp_* localStorage keys AND IndexedDB group state
Group cards show member count badge
Attempting to invite/create while offline shows a clear 'You are offline' message
All group UI passes mobile layout check at 375px
make build succeeds
make test-unit passes
make test-e2e passes (all specs)

includes: app/src/lib/marmot/syncQueue.ts: localStorage-backed queue for failed score syncs
app/src/lib/storage.ts: extend resetAllData to clear IndexedDB via idb-keyval

stories.notes: useOnlineStatus: return { isOnline, lastOnlineAt }. OfflineBanner: dismissible, re-shows when connectivity changes. syncQueue: on reconnect, drain the queue by calling publishScoreToGroups. resetAllData extension: call groupStorage.clearAll() which does idb-keyval del for all group-related keys. Mobile responsive: GroupCard and MemberList already use Chakra VStack/HStack so should be responsive, but audit at 375px.

dependency_notes: Story 01 establishes Nostr identity (required by all others). Story 02 creates the Marmot adapter (required by 03-07). Story 03 builds the groups UI shell (required by 04-07 for navigation). Story 04 completes the invite/join lifecycle (required by 05 for multi-user score sync). Story 05 adds score sync (required by 06 for the full groups experience). Story 06 adds backup (independent but builds on identity from 01). Story 07 polishes and hardens the whole feature.
mock_resolution_plan: Stories 01-02 have no UI dependencies. Story 03 uses MarmotContext directly (no mocks needed since 02 is done). Stories 04-07 build on the real implementations from prior stories. E2E tests for 04+ use a docker-compose strfry relay.
